task: SCOUT 1.7: Dolphin symbols for Mario Kart Wii

riptl_wii_symbols.description: Auto-generated SDK function symbols by pattern-matching known Wii SDK object files against the DOL binary. Covers RVL (Revolution/OS), NW4R (Nintendo Wii libraries), RevoEX (extended libraries), RVLDWC (DWC online). ~4,250 symbols per region.

snailspeed3_mkw_decomp.description: Active Mario Kart Wii decompilation project. config/RMCP01/symbols.txt contains 15,011 main DOL symbols with full type/size/scope info. config/RMCP01/module/symbols.txt contains 24,952 REL (dynamically loaded module) symbols with relative addresses. This is the most comprehensive symbol set available.

seeky_mkw_structures.description: Community-maintained pseudo-C++ documentation of MKWii structures, classes, and function signatures with addresses. Covers: racedata, player, raceinfo, itemhandler, itembehaviour, random, rknetcontroller, roomhandler, userhandler, selecthandler, eventhandler, itemslotdata, inputdata, miscpackethandler.

mkw_sp.description: Mario Kart Wii Service Pack mod. Contains partial source code with some address definitions, plus a linker script. Useful for cross-referencing function signatures.

dolphin_totaldb_dsy.description: Dolphin's built-in signature database (DSy format). Contains hash-based function signatures for Wii SDK functions. Not game-specific but useful for auto-detecting SDK functions in any Wii game.
dolphin_totaldb_dsy.note: Not downloaded (large binary format, SDK-level only)

mariokartwii_com_forum.description: Forum thread discussing the 4,251 symbol map (same as riptl/wii-symbols). References the riptl Gist and intns/mapdas tools.

intns_mapdas.description: Toolset for parsing Metrowerks .map files and Dolphin symbol maps. Can be used to generate new symbol maps from .map files.

wiigeckocodes.description: Gecko code database with many memory addresses used by cheat codes. Useful for finding specific game state addresses (not function symbols).

tockdom_wiki.description: MKWii community wiki with game internals documentation, file format specs, and some address tables.

notes: The snailspeed3/mkw symbols are by far the most comprehensive (39,963 symbols for RMCP01).
The riptl/wii-symbols cover 4 regions but only contain SDK-level function names (~4,250 each).
Module symbols (from REL files) use relative addresses and need base address adjustment at runtime.
Named (demangled C++) functions like nw4r::lyt::Draw*, EGG::*, RVL::* are the most valuable for reverse engineering.
The SeekyCt structure headers provide high-level class documentation that complements the raw symbol maps.
Addresses differ between regions: RMCP01 (US) and RMCJ01 (PAL) are close but RMCK01 (Korean) has more differences.
